what_it_does: This file is the verify-by-use completion-criteria scorecard for the recollection episodic-memory build loop. It lists verifiable statements about the system, grouped by component, each requiring real FUNCTION runs and real FORM/agent+operator verification before being marked complete.
observations: Defines four status states: ☐ unverified, ✅ verified-by-use, 🟡 code-complete-untested, 🔴 known-broken.
FUNCTION bar demands real runs — actual capture, recall, distill, MCP call — with no stubs or mocks.
FORM bar demands agent ergonomics, registry-visible/no-hardcoding, and design-system-based operator surfaces.
A standing 'no-fiction-about-Tim' gate applies to any principle/identity record.
Criteria are grouped G0 through G11, plus Wave-3 strengthened variants (★) and a dependency-first priority order.
Blocked criteria stay ☐ and the build leaves the seam; no green-painting over unanswered open decisions.
G10 bootstrap/north-star criteria require the build to use recollection to recover its own prior decisions and pass switch-flip/omniscience/no-fiction proofs.
G11 turns asserted laws into negative tests that must fail loud when rules are violated.
Wave-3 strengthened criteria explicitly tighten items a design audit flagged as weak.
standouts: The 'never ✅ from code-reading' rule and the dual FUNCTION + FORM bar make this a hard verification contract.
Negative-law floors (G11) reject malformed registry entries rather than silently accepting them.
No-fiction-about-Tim gate and abstention path are first-class, adversarially tested requirements.
Build-uses-itself (G10.1) and Pillar switch-flip/omniscience tests define success as measurable agent behavior.
Wave-3 strengthened items name the 'questionless-sweep lane' as the omniscience organ and require real population-scale edge graphs.
conventions: Uses structured markdown headings, grouped component sections, and checkbox status markers.
References external files by section: IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md, RESEARCH_SYNTHESIS.md, OPEN-DECISIONS.md.
Repeats cross-cutting concerns in every group: registry-visible/no-hardcoding, no-fiction, verify-by-use.
Uses ★ suffix to denote tightened Wave-3 criteria.
Pairs every functional claim with a form claim.
concerns: All checkboxes are ☐ in the snapshot, so the file reads as an unverified plan rather than a current state.
It depends heavily on external HOW/evidence/decision files that are not present here.
Some single lines bundle multiple verifiable claims, making pass/fail granularity ambiguous.
'Judged by a design-critic, not self-graded' introduces a subjective gate with no visible rubric.
References like D-2 and D-11 are unexplained from this file alone.
notes: This is the verify-by-use scorecard the recollection build loop must green before claiming each capability is real.
questions: What is the exact registry schema that backs 'adding a unit-type/lens/judge-type = a row'?
How is a 'real run' distinguished from a stub for each fabric/MCP/local-model criterion?
Who or what is the 'design-critic' for operator-surface form verification?
What do D-2 and D-11 refer to in OPEN-DECISIONS.md?
How are status updates audited and reconciled across the build loop?
apparent_themes: Verify by use, not by inspection
No-fiction and epistemic humility for identity/principle records
Bootstrap self-use as proof of utility
intention_signals: It tries to prevent premature 'green-painting' of memory features by forcing every claim to be demonstrated through real agent-facing use, while keeping seams open where decisions are unresolved and hardening the system against fabricated memory and silent recall failures.
novelty: A completion-criteria doc that forbids marking items complete from code-reading, requires both FUNCTION and FORM bars, includes negative tests for design laws, and has a bootstrap criterion where the build must query itself.
contribution: Provides a verification contract and dependency-ordered checklist that a fused build system can use to gate progress, preserve no-fiction/verify-by-use discipline, and leave open-decision seams ungreened.
summary_for_embedding: A verify-by-use completion criteria checklist for the recollection episodic memory build loop, grouping functional and form requirements from base schema through capture, distillation, lenses, links, gather/judge, recall surfaces, proactive injection, health, bootstrap proofs, and law-floor negative tests.
intention_for_embedding: To define the truth-table and dependency-first verification gates the recollection build loop must pass by real use before claiming each capability is complete.
